steps.title: Identify the greatest common factor
steps.content:
Look at the coefficients and variables in each term:
Coefficients: 8, 8, 88 have GCD = 8
Variables: $x^{10}$, $x^9$, $x$ have lowest power $x$
The GCF is 8x

steps.title: Factor out 8x and identify the choice
steps.content:
Divide each term by $8x$:
$$\frac{8x^{10}}{8x} = x^9$$
$$\frac{-8x^9}{8x} = -x^8$$
$$\frac{88x}{8x} = 11$$

The factored expression is:
$$8x(x^9 - x^8 + 11)$$

This matches choice D — D is correct.
